name: Hanauma Bay
tagline: A protected marine sanctuary inside a volcanic crater — the snorkeling is like swimming in an aquarium.
price: $25 non-resident, $5 kama'aina; closed Mondays and Tuesdays
parking: $3 parking; lot fills by 9 AM, arrive early or take the shuttle
hours: Wed–Sun, 6:45 AM – 4:00 PM (gates close at 1:30 PM for last entry)
bestTime: Book online reservation immediately when slots open (2 days in advance); Wed or Thu for fewest crowds
kamaaina: $5 entry for Hawaii residents with valid ID (no reservation needed for residents)
talkStory: Hanauma means 'curved bay' in Hawaiian — it formed inside a volcanic crater that eroded on one side, letting the ocean flood in thousands of years ago. Elvis Presley filmed scenes from Blue Hawaii here in 1961, cementing its fame. The mandatory conservation video before entry is no gimmick; the bay nearly died from overuse in the 1990s before strict limits brought the reef back. Go before 7 AM on a Wednesday for the emptiest experience, bring your own gear, and stay in the inner reef where 400+ fish species are so tame they brush against you.
